data.title: UDR declares $0.43 dividend
data.description:

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: FedEx beats Q1 consensus, sees FY26 revenue growth of 4-6%
Reviva Pharmaceuticals announces proposed public offering
data.description:
• Reviva Pharmaceuticals Holdings (NASDAQ:RVPH) on Thursday said it intends to offer shares of its common stock and warrants to purchase shares of common stock in a public offering.
  • All of the securities to be sold in the offering are to be offered by Reviva.
  • The company currently intends to use the net proceeds from the offering to fund research and development activities and for working capital and other general corporate purposes.
  • RVPH -21.31% after hours to $0.33.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: MGM Resorts International appoints Ayesha Molino COO
data.description:
Gaming and entertainment company MGM Resorts International (NYSE:MGM) announced on Thursday the appointment of Ayesha Molino as COO, effective January 1, 2026.

Molino will replace Corey Sanders, who had previously announced plans to retire on December 31, 2025.

Molino serves as the president and COO of ARIA and Vdara, responsible for all aspects of operations at two of the Las Vegas brands – AAA Five Diamond ARIA Resort & Casino and AAA Four Diamond Vdara Hotel & Spa.

data.title: Novo Nordisk reduces its U.S. sales team for obesity and diabetes education: Reuters
data.description:
Novo Nordisk (NVO) has laid off its U.S. sales team focused on obesity and diabetes education for healthcare providers, known as cardiometabolic educators, as part of broader restructuring under new CEO Maziar Mike Doustdar, Reuters said in a report. 

The team, consisting of several hundred employees, was notified last week that their roles would be terminated, though they will be paid until the end of the year.

These layoffs signal the Novo's effort to cut costs and regain its competitive edge against Eli Lilly (LLY) after losing dominance in the U.S. weight loss drug market and more than $400B in market value since mid-2024.

According to sources and LinkedIn posts found by Reuters, the U.S. cardiometabolic education team was entirely eliminated, and several affected employees are now seeking new positions.

data.title: FedEx rallies after topping profit expectations, updating on business split; UPS also higher
data.description:
FedEx Corporation (NYSE:FDX) rallied in post-market trading on Thursday after topping expectations with its FQ1 earnings report.

The Memphis-based shipping giant reported revenue was up 2.8% year-over-year to $22.2 billion in FQ1. EPS came in at $3.83 vs. $3.61 consensus and $3.60 a year ago.

Operating income was $1.30 billion during the quarter on an adjusted basis, vs. $1.22 billion consensus. The parcel delivery company reported an operating margin rate of 5.8% vs. 5.6% a year ago.

The company said Federal Express segment operating results improved during the quarter, driven by higher U.S. domestic and international priority package yields, continued cost savings from transformation initiatives, and increased U.S. domestic package volume. Those factors were partially offset by higher wage and purchased transportation rates, the impact of the evolving global trade environment on international export pac

FedEx Freight segment operating results decreased during the quarter due to lower revenue, higher wage rates, and the hiring of additional dedicated LTL sales professionals.

On the capital allocation front, FedEx completed $0.5 billion in share repurchases via open market transactions during the quarter. Approximately 2.2 million shares were repurchased, with the decrease in outstanding shares benefiting first-quarter results by $0.02 per diluted share. The company expects to continue repurchasing additional shares of common stock during the remainder of fiscal 2026. As of August 31, $1.6 billion remained available for repurchases under the company's 2024 stock repurchase authorization.

Notably, FedEx (NYSE:FDX) said the planned spin-off of FedEx Freight into a new publicly traded company continues to advance. The business split is expected to be achieved in a tax-efficient manner for FedEx (FDX) stockholders and executed by June 2026. Once separated, FedEx Freight will be a separately traded public company under the ticker symbol FDXF.

Looking ahead, FedEx sees FY26 revenue growth to be +4% to +6% and EPS of $17.20 to $19.00 (midpoint $18.10) vs. $18.36 consensus. "Our strategic initiatives, paired with our unique operational data platform from moving 17 million packages through our network daily, position us well to serve our customers in any environment and to create long-term value for our stockholders.," stated CEO Raj Subramaniam.

Shares of FedEx (FDX) were up 5.6% in after-hours trading on Thursday, after gaining 0.4% during the regular session. Peer UPS (UPS) was up 1.5% in the late session.

The ETFs with the most exposure to FedEx (FDX) by weighting are the iShares US Consumer Staples ETF (IYK), the Vanguard Consumer Staples Index Fund ETF (VDC), and the Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLP).

data.title: WTW increases share repurchase program by $1.5 billion
data.description:
• Willis Towers Watson (NASDAQ:WTW) on Thursday said that its Board of Directors has approved a $1.5 billion increase to its existing share repurchase program.

  • The new authorization adds to the approximately $200 million remaining under the current open-ended repurchase authority.
  • WTW will consider repurchasing shares based on factors including market conditions, legal requirements, and business considerations.
  • WTW +0.09% after hours to $331.68
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: JPMorgan Chase hits 52-week high, extends rally amid investor optimism on bank stocks
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) extended rally for the eighth consecutive trading session, hitting a 52-week high of $313.21 on Thursday, September 18.

The stock closed the day 0.46% higher at $313.18, demonstrating investor optimism around stocks from the banking sector.

As widely expected, the Federal Reserve trimmed its policy rate by 25 basis points to 4.00%-4.25% on Wednesday, and sees two more 25-bps cuts this year.

"Like small-caps and growth sectors, financials can benefit from cheaper capital. Lower interest rates help reduce the risk of loan defaults that happen in a high-interest-rate environment, improving banks' loan portfolios, and can help finance new investments," said Steven Cress, vice president of quantitative strategy and market data at Seeking Alpha.

Notably, U.S. bank stocks went up after the Fed announced a rate cut. The S&P 500 Index Banks Industry Group (SP500-4010) climbed 1.19% to close at 576.75 yesterday, against the broader S&P 500's 0.10% loss to 6,600.35.

Banking giant JPM, which has a market capitalization of $857.24B, added 6.27% over the course of the last seven trading days with investors pricing in a near-certain rate cut in the September FOMC meeting. 

The New York-based banking stock has been gaining since Tuesday, September 9.

Douglas Petno, co-CEO of the commercial and investment bank unit, estimated the bank's markets revenue for the second quarter to be up in about the high teens percentage rate during the Barclays 23rd Annual Global Financial Services Conference.

The stock closed the trading day 1.69% higher at $297.85.

Also, JPMorgan has also been in the news over hiring and reorganization since September 9. Recently, the bank reportedly appointed three senior investment bankers to its executive committee of global chairs for investment banking.

And, the bank was said to be looking to increase headcount at its corporate banking unit in the Asia Pacific region by 20% in 2026.

JPM is reportedly planning a new office building in Canary Wharf, London, to secure enough space to satisfy its growth ambitions in the region.

To add to the positive side, the company raised its quarterly dividend by 7.1% to $1.50 per share from the prior dividend of $1.40.

CFRA analyst Kenneth Leon upgraded the stock to Buy from Hold on the view that the bank is set to "accelerate growth and margins from businesses positioned in the capital markets." That includes trading, equity and debt underwriting, in addition to M&A advisory fees and fee growth from asset and wealth management.

The Wall Street analysts as well as Seeking Alpha authors are bullish on the stock.

JPMorgan Chase remains a very attractive Long Idea, combining industry-leading profitability, record profits, and a cheap valuation, according to Seeking Alpha author David Trainer, who owns the stock.

Meanwhile, Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating system grades the stock as Hold, with a score of 3.42 on a scale of 5. The quantitative rating system rates the stock as D for Valuation, C for Growth, F for Profitability, A for Momentum, and A+ for Revisions.

"This rally in JPM shares is entirely justified by its strong position and growth levers. However, these fundamental strengths are now largely reflected in valuation, meaning future returns are more likely to be market-like," said SA contributor Seeking Profits.

data.title: FedEx Non-GAAP EPS of $3.83 beats by $0.22, revenue of $22.2B beats by $550M
data.description:
• FedEx press release (NYSE:FDX): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $3.83 beats by $0.22.
  • Revenue of $22.2B (+2.8% Y/Y) beats by $550M.

  • For fiscal 2026, FedEx is forecasting:

    FedEx is reaffirming its fiscal 2026 forecast of:

    • A 4% to 6% revenue growth rate year over year;
    • Diluted earnings per share of $14.20 to $16.00 before the MTM retirement plans accounting adjustments, and $17.20 to $19.00 after also excluding costs related to business optimization initiatives, the planned spin-off of FedEx Freight, and the planned change in the company's fiscal year end;
    • An ETR of approximately 25% prior to the MTM retirement plans accounting adjustments; and
    • Pension contributions of up to $400 million, compared to the prior forecast of up to $600 million.
    • Permanent cost reductions of $1 billion in transformation-related savings from structural cost reductions and the advancement of Network 2.0; and
    • Capital spending of $4.5 billion, with a priority on investments in network optimization and efficiency improvement, including fleet and facility modernization and automation.
  • Shares +4.35 AH.

data.title: Darden updates 2026 sales growth outlook to 7.5%-8.5% amid strong Olive Garden and LongHorn expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Darden Restaurants, Inc. (DRI) Q1 2026

Management View

  • CEO Ricardo Cardenas stated that "we had a great quarter with same-restaurant sales and earnings growth that exceeded our expectations," highlighting that three of four segments delivered positive same-restaurant sales and traffic growth. Cardenas underscored Olive Garden's 5.9% same-restaurant sales increase, driven by new menu items and the growth of first-party delivery, and noted LongHorn Steakhouse's 5.5% same-restaurant sales growth, reinforcing the brand's #1 ranking in food quality, service, atmosphere, and value.
  • The CEO emphasized the successful test of a lighter portion section at Olive Garden, reporting "affordability scores increasing 15 percentage points and high satisfaction with portion size."
  • Cardenas provided an update on the sale of eight Olive Garden locations in Canada, stating, "we closed on the sale of those locations to Recipe Unlimited... and entered into an area development agreement to open 30 more Olive Garden's over the next 10 years."
  • CFO Rajesh Vennam reported, "sales and earnings growth exceeded our expectations as our sales momentum from the fourth quarter continued into the first quarter." He detailed that "we generated $3 billion of total sales, 10% higher than last year, driven by same-restaurant sales growth of 4.7%, the acquisition of 103 Chuy's restaurants and the addition of 22 net new restaurants."
  • Vennam added, "adjusted diluted net earnings per share from continuing operations of $1.97 were 12.6% higher than last year."

Outlook

  • Vennam announced, "we are raising our expected total sales growth and tightening the range of same-restaurant sales to reflect the outperformance in the first quarter, acceleration in our new unit pipeline and any incremental pricing we may take to partially offset the additional commodities costs."
  • Darden now expects total sales growth of 7.5% to 8.5% for fiscal 2026, same-restaurant sales growth of 2.5% to 3.5%, approximately 65 new restaurant openings, and total inflation of 3% to 3.5% with commodities inflation of 3% to 4%.
  • Full-year adjusted diluted net earnings per share guidance remains between $10.50 and $10.70.
  • Management stated, "we expect the lowest year-over-year EPS growth to be in the second quarter, driven by the significant step-up in beef costs and our measured approach to pricing for these costs."

Financial Results

  • Vennam noted, "Olive Garden delivered a strong segment profit margin of 20.6% for the quarter, which was only 10 basis points below last year, even with the investments in affordability and the impact of delivery fees."
  • LongHorn reported a segment profit margin of 17.4%, which CFO Vennam said was "60 basis points below last year" due to higher-than-expected beef costs.
  • The Other Business segment's sales increased 22.5%, with a segment profit margin of 16.1%, "90 basis points higher than last year."
  • The company returned $358 million to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases this quarter.

Q&A

  • Brian Harbour, Morgan Stanley, Research Division: Question on food cost visibility and beef price coverage. Vennam: "Right now, we only have about 25% coverage in beef for the next 6 months... beef is the biggest variable here."
  • Jon Tower, Citigroup Inc., Research Division: Asked about cost impact of affordability and delivery at Olive Garden. Vennam: "They were roughly on the margin, if you just purely look at the margin percentage impact, they are probably about 20 basis points each."
  • David Palmer, Evercore ISI: Questioned sustainability of casual dining momentum and Olive Garden's strategy for tougher compares. Cardenas replied that casual dining value perception and plans for continued menu innovation and affordability would help sustain growth.
  • James Salera, Stephens Inc.: Requested LongHorn's traffic and ticket breakdown. Vennam: "LongHorn traffic was up about 3.2%... same-restaurant sales were 5.5%. So the check was 2.3%. Their pricing was 2.5%."
  • Jake Bartlett, Truist Securities: Asked about Olive Garden delivery mix and promotion impact. Cardenas: "For Olive Garden delivery in the first quarter was about 5%. We exited at about 4%."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on costs, delivery incrementality, and strategic pricing, with a slightly positive tone driven by confidence in market share gains and new initiatives. Some skepticism surfaced regarding beef cost headwinds and promotional strategy.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, especially in prepared remarks, using phrases such as "I am pleased with the strong start to our new fiscal year" and "we feel really good about our development pipeline." In Q&A, management was forthright about beef cost uncertainty but continued to express confidence in long-term strategy.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remained consistently confident, with more emphasis this quarter on navigating commodity volatility and leveraging menu innovation for traffic growth.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for total sales growth increased from 7%-8% to 7.5%-8.5%, and the same-restaurant sales growth range tightened and edged upward.
  • Strategic focus continued on affordability and value, with Olive Garden's lighter portion menu test and continued investment in delivery and marketing.
  • Management maintained confidence in long-term strategy, echoing last quarter's focus on reinvestment for growth rather than short-term margin expansion.
  • Analysts’ questions shifted more toward commodity inflation and delivery incrementality, while still monitoring traffic and unit growth acceleration.
  • Key financial metrics such as Olive Garden and LongHorn margins and traffic remained in focus, with updates on the positive impact of menu and service innovations.

Risks and Concerns

  • Vennam identified beef cost volatility as a primary risk, explaining, "we only have about 25% coverage in beef for the next 6 months."
  • Tariffs on shrimp and supply constraints in beef were highlighted as additional inflationary pressures.
  • Management acknowledged promotional investments and delivery fees could create margin pressure but reiterated discipline in pricing and investment decisions.
  • Analysts questioned the sustainability of current traffic trends, the impact of smaller portion tests, and the short-term effect of commodity cost increases on earnings.

Final Takeaway

Darden Restaurants delivered another quarter of sales and earnings growth above expectations, driven by strong performance at Olive Garden and LongHorn Steakhouse and continued strategic investments in menu innovation, affordability, and delivery. The company raised its fiscal 2026 sales growth outlook and remains focused on disciplined pricing, unit expansion, and navigating commodity inflation, while maintaining confidence in its ability to drive long-term value for shareholders.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
